{
  "source_linx": "439281ce-4c81-4c57-a1a6-aeda65dde2ec",
  "original_content": "I hate leetcode, it feels useless for real work",
  "meta_insight": {
    "pattern": "Frustration and resistance arising from a perceived disconnect between required effort/skills (e.g., LeetCode) and their practical utility or direct relevance to professional goals ('real work').",
    "principle": "Humans are driven by a need for perceived utility and relevance in their tasks, especially when significant effort is required. A lack of perceived practical application leads to demotivation and negative emotional responses.",
    "strategy": "When introducing or requiring tasks that might seem abstract or academic, explicitly articulate their real-world applications, underlying principles, or long-term benefits to enhance motivation and mitigate resistance.",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "tags": [
      "motivation",
      "utility",
      "relevance",
      "frustration",
      "learning",
      "skill transfer",
      "engagement",
      "interview prep"
    ]
  },
  "patterns": [
    {
      "pattern_type": "emotional/cognitive",
      "description": "Frustration and demotivation arise when required effort (e.g., LeetCode) is perceived as disconnected or irrelevant to the desired practical outcome (e.g., real work performance). This is a pattern of perceived low utility or poor transfer of learning.",
      "instances": [
        "d3ae77fa-8db6-4fa4-ab32-1e834ca581ea",
        "439281ce-4c81-4c57-a1a6-aeda65dde2ec"
      ],
      "strength": 1.0
    }
  ],
  "strategy": {
